data.title: Nanalysis upsizes private placement, closes first tranche
data.description:
• Nanalysis Scientific (NSCI:CA) said on Tuesday it has closed the first tranche of a previously announced non-brokered private placement, raising about $2.5 million in gross proceeds.
  • The company issued roughly 16.5 million units at $0.15 per unit and said the proceeds will be used for debt reduction. It expects to close a second tranche in January 2026.
  • Nanalysis also said it has increased the size of the offering due to strong investor demand, allowing for the issuance of up to an additional 23,333,333 units for total gross proceeds of up to $3.5 million, subject to approval by the TSX Venture Exchange.
  • Additional funds will be used for debt reduction and magnet inventory purchases.
  • NSCIF closed -0.0% at $0.115.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ARMOUR Residential REIT reports Q4 results
data.description:
• ARMOUR Residential REIT press release (ARR): Q4 The Company ended the quarter with revenues, $210.2M
  • net income to common,$156.3M
  • EPS of $1.49.
  • ARR also saw impressive economic net interest income of $91.6M, which is an increase from the $65.4M posted last year.
  • Economic interest rate was 5.0% for the quarter and increased from 4.78% last quarter while economic interest expense was 3.17%, a smaller increase from 2.96% last quarter.
  • This led to an economic interest spread of 1.83%. Distributable earnings for the quarter were $75.3M or $0.72 per share.
  • Distributable EPS declined 7.0% sequentially and 28.3% year over year. Finally, the Company ended the quarter with a BV/Share of $17.49 which is an increase of 3.5% compared to $16.90 at the end of last quarter. This gain in BV, combined with common dividends, resulted in a total economic return of 7.75% for the quarter.
  •  Armour was once again a strong dividend payer in the quarter. Total dividend payments were $0.72 per common share in the quarter, which is a monthly rate of $0.24.

data.title: Ramaco announces $100 million stock repurchase plan
data.description:
• Ramaco Resources (METC) announced on Tuesday that the board of directors has authorized the initiation of a share repurchase program of up to $100 million of the currently outstanding shares of its Class A common stock over a period of 24 months.
  • The Board also authorized the company to enter into written trading plans under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, to facilitate the repurchase of its common stock pursuant to its share repurchase program.
  • "We're excited to introduce a share repurchase program as another means of returning capital to our shareholders, which we are introducing as a matter of good corporate governance," said Randall Atkins, Chairman and CEO of Ramaco. "With the closing of Ramaco's previously-announced $600 plus million (before fees) in capital raises in the second half of 2025, we are well-positioned to pursue opportunities to unlock value and maximize shareholder returns. The share repurchase program highlights our confidence in our continued operational performance as well as financial strength."
  • The company said it intends to purchase shares under the repurchase program opportunistically with available funds, while maintaining sufficient liquidity to fund its capital development program.
  • METC +3.86% after hours to $17.48.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Data Storage extends expiration time of offer to purchase
data.description:
• Data Storage (DTST) announced on Tuesday that it has extended the expiration time of its offer to purchase to January 12, 2026, from the previously scheduled January 7, 2026.
  • The Company will file Amendment No. 2 to its Schedule TO, extending the tender offer expiration to January 12, 2026, at 12:00 midnight (New York City time), unless extended or terminated earlier.

data.title: CASI Pharmaceuticals wins Nasdaq extension to meet listing rules
data.description:
• CASI Pharmaceuticals (CASI) said on Tuesday it has received an extension from a Nasdaq hearings panel to remain listed, giving the company until February 17, 2026 to regain compliance with the exchange’s minimum market value of listed securities requirement.
  • The exchange had previously notified the company that it was out of compliance with the $35 million threshold after its market value fell below the requirement for 30 consecutive trading days.
  • CASI was initially given until November 3, 2025 to regain compliance.
  • The panel granted the extension after reviewing steps taken by the company, including management changes, financing actions, a convertible note purchase agreement, additional funding initiatives, and plans to divest its China assets.
  • The extension stays any suspension of CASI’s securities while it works to meet the requirement.
  • CASI -1.73% after hours to $0.7587.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bessent sees GDP jump leading to increased hiring, 'large paychecks'
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a post on Tuesday that the better-than-expected Q3 GDP growth, under the Trump administration, will lead to "a significant increase in hiring in the months ahead and the large paychecks that will inevitably accompany these jobs."

The real GDP growth, excluding government, rose 4.7% at an annualized rate over the last two quarters, he said. 

"This is the exact opposite of what happened under Biden, when government growth contributed to historic inflation and depressed living standards," he wrote in a post on X.

The headline GDP number indicated a 4.3% annualized rate of growth in Q3, up from +3.8% in Q2, the Bureau of Economic Analysis said on Tuesday. That exceeded the consensus estimate of +3.2%.

Earlier, Bessent suggested that the Federal Reserve consider revisiting its 2% inflation target, noting that a range would be better.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: S&P 500 notched a record close as Wall Street ended higher after GDP data
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages closed higher on Tuesday and the S&P 500 notched its highest recorded close of 6,909.79.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended +0.5%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed +0.4%, and the blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) finished +0.1%.

From a sector vantage point, seven of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors closed in the green with Communication Services at the top. At the other end, Consumer Staples was the weakest perfomer.

“We are now entering the season when end-of-year tax-related selling and window-dressing buying will compete. The early indicators point to higher stocks, stable bonds, and rip-roaring bullish precious metals, all in the spotlight. Silver traded above $70 per ounce today, while gold rose above $4,500. Platinum is within a few bucks of the 2008 record high,” Seeking Alpha analyst Andrew Hecht noted.

“The stock market is finally starting to eke out some gains for December after a choppy few weeks, and just in time for the market's Santa Claus rally, which we expect to take place in its typical format via the last several trading days of the year,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer of Granite Bay Wealth Management said. 

“The combination of low volume and an absence of bad news should keep the Santa Claus rally alive and well for the rest of 2025,” Stanley added. 

The Santa Claus rally refers to a historical trend that has seen the benchmark S&P 500 index post gains during the last five trading days of the year and the first two of the next year. 

On the economic calendar, U.S. Q3 GDP annualized growth of +4.3% blew past the consensus figure of +3.2%. However, delving past the topline beat, the report showed some weakness. 

"The main issue in the Q3 data is the strong performance of household consumption against weak real incomes. Ex-transfers, real income is flat over the last two quarters while consumption is expanding 3%. Either income is being underreported or we're seeing an unsustainable drawdown in savings," Renaissance Macro Research said on X. 

Furthermore, durable goods orders fell more than expected in the month of October. 

U.S. Treasury yields advanced. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 2 basis points to 3.53%, while the U.S.10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.17%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Marvell Technology (MRVL) closed +3.3% while Robinhood Markets (HOOD) ended -1.7%.

data.title: Limoneira Company GAAP EPS of -$0.49 misses by $0.38, revenue of $42.82M beats by $7.47M
data.description:
• Limoneira Company press release (LMNR): GAAP EPS of -$0.49 misses by $0.38.
  • Revenue of $42.82M beats by $7.47M.

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Guidance and Longer-Term Outlook

    The Company expects fresh lemon volumes to be in the range of 4.0 million to 4.5 million cartons for fiscal year 2026. Avocado volumes are expected to be in the range of 5.0 million to 6.0 million pounds for fiscal year 2026.

    The Company expects to receive total proceeds of approximately $180 million from Harvest, LLCB II, LLC and East Area II spread out over seven fiscal years, of which $10 million was received in April 2025 and $15 million was received in fiscal year 2024.

data.title: Bank of Canada officials unsure about direction of next move
data.description:
The Bank of Canada's Governing Council said the high level of uncertainty that the Canadian economy is facing makes it "difficult to predict when and in which direction the next change in the policy rate would be," according to the summary of the officials' deliberations at their December meeting.

Recall that Canada's central bank kept its policy rate unchanged at 2.25% earlier this month. At the time, the council considered the policy rate to be at the lower end of the Bank's estimate of neutral — the point at which interest rates neither stimulate nor depress economic growth — which was appropriate to provide some support as the economy works through structural transition.

Much of the uncertainty revolves around the shifting trade outlook ahead of the review of the Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement, the same as what the U.S. calls the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement. That "would likely weigh on business investment," the BoC said in the summary.

The council members discussed the two ends of the spectrum for the CUSMA negotiations outcome. "A worst-case scenario involving the dissolution of CUSMA and higher tariffs would be very damaging to the Canadian economy," the summary said.  "Alternatively, a resolution of CUSMA negotiations that provided some stability in North American trade policy could spur on business investment."

The policymakers committed to assessing incoming data to determine the next move and to be nimble. "If a significant new shock were to materialize, or an accumulation of evidence indicated that the evolution of economic activity and inflation was materially different from what they expected, Governing Council was prepared to respond," the minutes said.

data.title: Bessent signals openness to revisiting Fed's 2% inflation goal
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has said that the Federal Reserve could revisit its 2% inflation target after consumer price growth is firmly under control, warning that moving too soon could hurt credibility.

“Once we are back to 2 — which I think will be in sight — then we can have a discussion: Is it much smarter to have a range?” Bessent said in an interview on the All-In Podcast, as quoted by Bloomberg. “Once we re-anchor to the target, then we can talk about a range.”

The Treasury chief suggested that the future inflation-targeting ranges could be 1.5%-2.5% or 1%-3%. 

The notion of “decimal-point certainty is just absurd,” Bessent said in the interview, which was posted on Dec. 22 and recorded after the Dec. 18 release of the November CPI report. Still, he warned that changing the objective while price growth remains elevated could signal a willingness to move up the goalposts whenever inflation runs too hot, a risk to the Fed's credibility.

Bessent acknowledged that consumer price levels remain elevated, although he emphasized inflation is starting to ease, helped in part by falling rents.
